_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 16:02:38Z)**_

This change is in `docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx` — a Vercel-hosted visual mockup that renders only hardcoded demo data (`admins`, `VESSELS`, `VIEWER_ROLES`, `COLORS`).

Phase 1 (entry points/sinks): No HTTP handlers, no IPC, no eval/exec, no SQL, no FS, no outbound HTTP, no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. The change adds two JSX branches that render badges from constants.

Phase 2 (data flow):
- `a.role`, `a.port`, `a.assignedVessels`, `a.name`, `a.email` come from the in-file mockup `admins` array — not user input.
- `label` is built from `shortName(list[0])` where `list` is filtered via `VESSELS.includes(v)` (a hardcoded allowlist of vessel name constants), and rendered as a React child (auto-escaped). No injection sink.
- `style={{ background: '#DCFCE7', color: COLORS.success }}` — all CSS values are literals or constants, no attacker-controlled CSS-in-JS.

Phase 2b/c: No parser, no IaC, no CI/CD trigger, no allowlist gating a sink, no permission grant, no logging of secrets, no DDL — the diff is a pure presentational branch in a static demo.

No realistic attacker surface exists in this file (it's a static mockup served to view-only viewers). No findings.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 16:02:43Z)**_

No security findings. The change is purely presentational in a static React mockup file (`docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`):

- All rendered values (`a.role`, `a.port`, `a.assignedVessels`, vessel labels) come from hardcoded mockup data, not user input.
- The new `label` string is built from items filtered through `VESSELS.includes(v)` (a hardcoded allowlist) and rendered as a React child (auto-escaped) — no XSS sink.
- `style={...}` values are all literal hex strings or `COLORS.*` constants — no CSS injection vector.
- No new sinks added (no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no eval, no shell, no SQL, no FS, no outbound HTTP, no IPC, no auth/permission logic).

Findings: `[]`.
